source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A cylindrical container is separated into two compartments by a thin piston. The left compartment is filled with one mole of helium gas; the right compartment is filled with argon gas. These gases may be considered ideal. The cylinder is submerged in 1 liter of water, and the entire system is initially at the uniform temperature of 25\^\circ C. The heat capacities of the cylinder and piston may be neglected. When the piston is unclamped, a new equilibrium situation is ultimately reached with the piston in a new position. How far from the left end of the cylinder will the piston come to rest?
